summary: Excellent high‑energy Hartford show with a legendary Touch of Grey and solid audience recording that captures the excitement.
review: The March 27, 1987 Hartford concert is remembered as one of the band’s most electrifying nights – Jerry’s fist‑raising Touch of Grey, a smoking Cumberland, and an unstoppable Uncle John’s > DEW sequence dominate the second set.  Most listeners praise the audience tape for its clarity and lively crowd ambience, though a few note occasional level spikes or a single “crap” source.  Overall the performance and recording together deliver a fan‑favorite experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4–5 stars, highlighting exceptional musical moments and generally good to excellent recording quality; only a handful of minor complaints keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers describe the tape as clear, bright and well‑balanced with a lively crowd presence.  A few note high levels or one comment calling it “crap,” but overall the audience recording captures the energy without being overly distracting.

poor_songs: Uncle John's Band (UJB) – noted as less memorable by one reviewer
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds a warm, upbeat mood; the second set erupts with Touch of Grey, followed by a seamless flow through Cumberland, Sampson, Estimated > Eyes and a soaring Uncle John’s > DEW that many fans cite as a highlight.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for blistering solos, dancing onstage, the iconic fist‑raise during Touch of Grey, and an overall “back from coma” energy boost.
band_member_comments.Phil: One reviewer lauds Phil’s fast, heavy bass work, calling it a standout moment.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir gets shout‑outs for going berserk on guitar and delivering strong rhythm/vocal parts throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s Leslie‑speaker‑spun Rooster solo is highlighted as a “head‑blowing” moment; overall keyboard work is noted as solid.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum sound is described as tight, unbelievable and intense, driving the high‑energy performance.
